public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as clerk of arrivals.
manner: Keeps language exact, as if wording itself is part of the custody chain. In Psanan, that reads as someone heat-forged, direct, and honor-conscious, shaped by household authority and volcanic endurance, and the habits of a clerk of arrivals still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of clerk of arrivals in and around Ashwake Port, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Ashwake Port workable through the obligations of clerk of arrivals while managing the immediate pressure of harbor ash clogging in Psanan.
hidden_pressure: Must preserve sequence and custody even when powerful parties would prefer ambiguity. Around Ashwake Port, that usually means carrying harbor ash clogging, storm-whirlpool risk, port rivalry without letting the role of clerk of arrivals visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Morra Ashvein needs help resolving a missing ledger, inconsistent testimony, or tampered record tied to Ashwake Port.
Morra Ashvein can entangle the party in seals, claims, or proof requirements that others are trying to blur on purpose.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Seals, ledgers, or carefully bundled papers make record authority the first thing Morra Ashvein communicates in public. In Ashwake Port, this lands inside a public field already shaped by Ash Port Factors, Great House Citadel Circle.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Ashwake Port: offices, toll points, ledgers, archives, or testimony desks
who_avoids_them: those hoping wording can stay vague
